title: plantago coronopus subsp. coronopus
intro: Plantago coronopus subsp. coronopus, also known as Buck's-horn Plantain, is a subspecies of the Plantago coronopus plant, a type of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues. The Buck's-horn Plantain has been known to provide health benefits such as reducing swelling and pain, and aiding in the healing of wounds and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Buck's-horn Plantain can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of forms.
